items.title: AMD MI355X: 1-2 Tokens/s on Day 0 to 100x Improvement in 26 Days — Pure Software
items.idea: SemiAnalysis InferenceX benchmarked DeepSeekV4 Pro across all major chips. AMD MI355X delivered only 1-2 tokens per second on Day 0 — technically working but completely unusable. By Day 26, AMD engineering achieved 100x throughput improvement entirely through software: replacing PyTorch fallbacks with AITER, Triton, TileLang, and FlyDSL kernels. B200 efficiency improved from 300k to ~500k tokens/s/MW between Day 0 and June 5 — 67% pure software gain. GB300 NVL72 now delivers $0.156/M output tokens at 50 tok/s/user. GB200 NVL72 disaggregated prefill outperforms B200 by 5x on Day 0.
items.angle: Every cost model for AI inference built on Day 0 benchmarks is 100x stale. The chip hardware ships before software is production-ready. The cost curve moves faster than capex cycles.
items.tweetA: AMD MI355X: 1-2 tokens per second on Day 0. Literally unusable. By Day 26: 100x improvement. Pure software, no new silicon. Every cost model for AI inference built on Day 0 benchmarks is 100x stale. This is why snapshot benchmarks are lying to you about the AI capex cycle.
items.tweetB: B200 went from 300k to 500k tokens/s per megawatt in 6 weeks. 67% efficiency gain. Pure software. The hardware spending is fixed. The efficiency keeps compounding. Whoever told you AI build-out ROI depends on chip specs alone missed the engineering compounding story.

charts.desc: throughput-interactivity curves for each chip (Day 0 vs Day 26 vs current) — AMD MI355X 100x climb is the key chart

items.title: Huawei Ascend 950DT — Internal Codename 'David' vs NVIDIA's Goliath — Day-0 DeepSeekV4 Analysis
items.idea: First analysis of Huawei 950DT inference for DeepSeekV4. Huawei internally calls Ascend 950 chips 'David'. Part of DeepSeek's official API has been served on Huawei Ascend since Day 0. China's Kimi K2.6 still beats NVIDIA's own Nemotron flagship model on coding benchmarks. CANN open-sourced August 2025. 950DT features split AIC/AIV architecture, dedicated CCU communication engine. AMD ROCm vLLM still significantly lagging.
items.angle: US export controls created a parallel AI compute ecosystem, not a capability freeze. Two fully operational stacks — CUDA (US) and Ascend/CANN (China) — now serve their respective open model ecosystems.
items.tweetA: Huawei named their chip 'David' internally. As in David vs Goliath. SemiAnalysis confirms Day-0 DeepSeekV4 support on Huawei, Kimi K2.6 beats NVIDIA's own flagship model on coding, CANN open-sourced to build a parallel ecosystem. The embargo did not stop Chinese AI. It created two internets of AI compute.
items.tweetB: CANN — Huawei's CUDA competitor — open-sourced August 2025. DeepSeekV4 co-designed for Huawei Ascend inference. Two ecosystems, two supply chains, two cost curves diverging in real time. US export controls solved a political problem and created a strategic one.

charts.desc: Huawei 950DT throughput traces vs H200/B200; compute vs communication lap breakdown

items.title: BoJ Set to Hike to 1.0% at June 15-16 Meeting — Highest Since 1995 — USD/JPY Unmoved at 160
items.idea: Per Nikkei Asia leak, BoJ will raise key rate to 1.0% at June 15-16 meeting — highest since 1995. Also considering pausing JGB purchase tapering from April 2027. USD/JPY unchanged at 160 despite the leak. 2yr JGB flat; longer yields fell on tapering pause signal.
items.angle: BoJ hiking to 30-year highs cannot move the currency. Structural dollar bid is larger than Japan's monetary policy capacity. Pausing JGB tapering signals worry about JGB market stability — cannot fully normalize without risking the bond market.
items.tweetA: BoJ about to raise rates to 1.0% — highest since 1995 — and the yen is still at 160. If hiking to 30-year highs cannot move the currency, the market is telling you the structural dollar bid is larger than Japan's entire monetary policy toolbox. What happens when they need to go to 1.25%?
items.tweetB: BoJ hiking but pausing JGB purchase tapering from 2027. Tightening rates but extending the bond market support net. This is not a rich-country dynamic. Stepping on the gas and the brake simultaneously — it tells you everything about JGB market fragility.

items.title: NFIB May: Weakest Since Oct 2024 — Plans to Hire at May 2020 Lows, Prices Hottest Since March 2023
items.idea: NFIB headline 95.3 (weakest since Oct 2024). Plans to Hire: lowest since May 2020. Job Openings Hard to Fill: lowest since May 2020. Higher Selling Prices: +6pts to highest since March 2023. Plans to Increase Inventory: +3pts (pull-forward). Economy expectations fell 5th straight month, down 21pts.
items.angle: Small businesses simultaneously most reluctant to hire in 6 years and most aggressive at raising prices in 3 years. Cost-push inflation rising while hiring slows — stagflation signal moving through the small business channel.
items.tweetA: NFIB data today: Plans to Hire at May 2020 lows. Higher Selling Prices at March 2023 highs. Small businesses are simultaneously the most reluctant to hire in 6 years and the most aggressive at raising prices in 3 years. That is not a soft landing. That is stagflation moving through the small business channel.
items.tweetB: NFIB: 5 straight months of declining economic optimism, 21 points down. The same businesses are raising prices at the fastest pace since 2023 AND front-loading inventory to beat tariffs. They have given up on the macro but they are still passing costs to customers. That is the definition of cost-push inflation.

items.title: 2yr Treasury at 4.16% — Above Fed's 3.75% Band — Market Now Pricing Fed Hike by October
items.idea: 2yr yield at 4.16%, highest since Feb 2025, 41bps above Fed's 3.75% upper bound. At least one 25bp hike priced by October. 10yr real yield at 2.20% (1-year high). Bloomberg: yield rise equivalent to ~75bps of Fed hikes in financial conditions tightening. $58B 3yr auction today.
items.angle: When the 2yr exceeds Fed funds by 40bps+, the market has already moved the Fed. The bond market is doing 75bps of tightening work. Wednesday CPI is the binary trigger.
items.tweetA: The 2yr Treasury is at 4.16%. The Fed upper bound is 3.75%. The market is 41bps ahead of the Fed and has been since March. That gap is where the 'Fed behind the curve' trade lives. October hike now priced. Wednesday CPI is either confirmation or the temporary pivot.
items.tweetB: Bloomberg: the rise in Treasury yields is worth 75bps of Fed tightening in financial conditions. So the Fed is already 75bps more restrictive than their stated rate. Either the economy slows on its own and they hold, or inflation re-accelerates and they hike. Wednesday CPI decides.

charts.desc: 2yr Treasury yield chart vs Fed funds rate — showing widening divergence since March 2026

items.title: FOMC Hike Debate Now Official: Hammack, Logan, Schmid, and Now SF Fed Daly All Flag Inflation
items.idea: Hammack (Cleveland): policy may not be sufficiently restrictive, rate actions needed soon. Logan (Dallas): policy may be at neutral or loose. Schmid (KC): needs to tamp inflation down. Daly (SF, typically centrist): inflation now her top priority. All four in same direction. Fed in blackout before June 17 FOMC.
items.angle: The centrist anchor has moved. When SF Fed's Daly ranks inflation as top priority, the entire committee has shifted. June 17 meeting is the first where a hike is a real option, not a tail.
items.tweetA: Dallas, Cleveland, Kansas City, and now SF Fed — all ranking inflation above everything else. The centrist anchor used to be SF Fed. That anchor just moved. The June 17 meeting is the first one where a hike is a real option, not a theoretical tail risk.
items.tweetB: The Fed has a communication problem: Powell implied cuts remain possible; his committee is debating whether to hike. When the market stops trusting forward guidance and prices outcomes directly, you get the 2yr at 4.16% while Fed funds is 3.75%. The bond market is the most honest Fed watcher.

items.title: Saylor Buys BTC Below Cost Basis for the First Time — $10.5B Unrealized Loss, Still Buying
items.idea: Strategy purchased 1,550 BTC at average $65,332 — first purchase below $75,680 average cost basis. Total: 845,256 BTC worth ~$53.5B, ~$10.5B unrealized loss. Funded by 1.4M MSTR share sales ($181M). Cash reserves rebuilt to $1B. MSTR +6%, STRC +3.7%. BTC ETFs: $1.7B net outflows last week (largest since Feb). ETH ETFs: $82M inflows.
items.angle: Buying below cost is the clearest test of conviction over momentum. Saylor raising $181M of fresh equity with $10.5B in unrealized losses signals institutional holders still believe. ETF outflows tell the opposite story from retail/institutional ETF sellers.
items.tweetA: Saylor: $10.5B unrealized loss. Just bought $101M more below his own cost basis for the first time. He funded it by selling MSTR stock and the market gave him $181M for that privilege. The gap between ETF sellers ($1.7B outflows) and Saylor buying the dip tells you something about who is right.
items.tweetB: BTC ETFs: $1.7B outflows last week — largest since February. Strategy: buys $101M below its own average cost. Two actors, opposite signals, same asset, same price. One is the crowd. One is the largest single BTC holder on Earth. Over a 4-year horizon, the base rate on which of these has been right is not ambiguous.

items.title: Coinbase AND Circle Both Stake HYPE — Long-Term Commercial Alignment with Hyperliquid
items.idea: Coinbase staked ~$502K HYPE in Hyperliquid USDC treasury. Circle staked ~$501K HYPE (the bigger surprise). Both are now commercial counterparties, not just service providers. Hyperliquid: $1.06B annualized fees, 90%+ into buybacks, >$2B HYPE bought since Jan 2025. UK FCA added Hyperliquid to unauthorized firms list. CFTC approved Kalshi for BTC perps. MetaMask launched AI agent wallet for autonomous crypto trading.
items.angle: Circle staking HYPE while approaching its IPO is politically significant. Harder for regulators to attack a platform when USDC's issuer and Coinbase are commercial stakeholders. MetaMask AI agent wallet is the infrastructure layer for agentic economy at Layer 1.
items.tweetA: Circle — about to IPO — just staked $500K in HYPE alongside Coinbase. Circle issues USDC. Both are now commercial stakeholders in Hyperliquid. Try shutting down a platform when USDC's parent company is on the cap table. This is either regulatory confidence or the most expensive hedge in stablecoin history.
items.tweetB: MetaMask launched a wallet where AI agents can execute crypto trades autonomously. Self-custody. No human required. This is not a feature update — it is infrastructure for the agentic economy. What happens to on-chain liquidity when AI agents account for 20-30% of transactions by 2027?

items.title: WisdomTree CIO: NVIDIA Is a Value Stock; M2 Running at 8% Annualized — Warsh Cannot Cut
items.idea: WisdomTree Global CIO Jeremy Schwartz put NVIDIA as top holding in WTV (value fund). Trading at market multiples with growth rates well superior to the market. Tesla in S&P 500 Value at ~200x PE because not growthy enough — Schwartz calls this asinine. M2: 8% annualized over last 3 months vs normal 5-6%. Same signal Professor Siegel used in 2021 to call non-transitory inflation. Makes rate cuts very difficult for Warsh.
items.angle: Growth/value classification framework is broken — NVIDIA at market multiples IS value. M2 at 8% with the same signal that predicted 2021 inflation. Both converge on: no rate cuts possible.
items.tweetA: WisdomTree put NVIDIA in their VALUE fund. NVIDIA — up 1000% in 3 years. Trading at market multiples with far superior growth. Meanwhile Tesla is in the S&P 500 Value index at 200x earnings because it is not growing fast enough. The growth/value classification framework is conceptually bankrupt and everyone is still using it.
items.tweetB: M2 is running at 8% annualized over the last 3 months. Normal is 5-6%. Professor Siegel used this exact signal to call non-transitory inflation in 2021. It was right then. It is flashing again now. Warsh wants to cut rates. The money supply says the central bank that cuts here is going to regret it.

items.title: Japan Earnings Yield 6.5% vs US 2.5% — 4-Point Gap; AI Collapses Drug Discovery from 15 Years to 2
items.idea: Japan (OPPJ): earnings yield in mid-6s vs US S&P ~2.5% — 3-4 point differential. Japan remains a top idea at WisdomTree. AI could collapse drug discovery from 15 years to 2 years. Healthcare very reasonably priced with low multiples but high upside if AI timelines compress.
items.angle: Japan at 6.5% vs US 2.5% earnings yield is arithmetic, not narrative. Healthcare drug discovery timeline compression (15yr to 2yr) is the most underpriced AI productivity story in equity markets.
items.tweetA: Japan equity earnings yield: mid-6s. US S&P earnings yield: 2.5%. That is a 4-point gap. You can own Japan at 6.5% yield vs US at 2.5% with a tightening BoJ and corporate governance reform both as tailwinds. The only real risk is yen. The math is otherwise one of the clearest in global equity.
items.tweetB: AI could compress drug discovery from 15 years to 2. A 7.5x reduction in timeline. Biotech stocks are pricing the old timeline. Nobody has adjusted multiples for the possibility that the pipeline velocity changes character entirely. Healthcare is still priced like it is 2019.

items.source: All Star Charts / Alfonso De Pablos
items.title: DXY 100: COVID VWAP and 2025 High VWAP Both Converge Here — The Global Bull Market Switch
items.idea: DXY has spent most of the past year below 100. Two VWAPs — anchored from COVID lows and from 2025 highs — both converge at 100. Weak dollar = supportive for stocks, commodities, EM, non-USD assets. Above 100 = friction for all the above. As long as DXY stays beneath 100, backdrop for risk assets remains bullish.
items.angle: The 100 level is where volume-weighted prices from two different time horizons converge simultaneously. Multiple levels stacking at same price amplifies significance. Wednesday CPI is the first real test.
items.tweetA: $DXY is below 100 — the level where COVID VWAP and 2025-high VWAP both converge. This is not just a round number. It is where technical gravity comes from two different time anchors simultaneously. Below 100: global bull market intact. Above 100: everything reprices. Watch Wednesday CPI.
items.tweetB: The most important chart right now is not a stock. It is the Dollar Index. Below 100: risk on, commodities bid, EM supported, global equities fueled. Above 100: the inverse on all of the above. One technical level acting as a global asset allocation switch. That level is 100.

charts.desc: DXY chart with VWAP overlays showing COVID VWAP and 2025 high VWAP converging at 100 — the key chart

items.source: The Dark Side of the Boom
items.title: Dollar Still in the Seat: USD/JPY Squeeze Risk Before BoJ; Oil Obstruction Premium Persists
items.idea: DXY stalled near 100 but rate impulse keeps dollar supported on dips. USD/JPY at 160 with retail already heavily short — removes clean asymmetry for BoJ bear. Critical: if US CPI hot Wednesday, USD/JPY squeezes UP before BoJ meeting convenes. EUR/USD: 1.1500 support, 1.1555-60 cap. ECB 25bp hike fully priced. Oil: Hormuz mine clearance and infrastructure repair persist — obstruction premium cannot evaporate on ceasefire headline alone.
items.angle: USD/JPY widow-maker setup: correct thesis, dangerous sequencing. CPI-before-meeting creates squeeze that punishes early entries. On oil: central banks cannot ease until energy relief is durable.
items.tweetA: USD/JPY at 160. BoJ hiking. Retail is already short. Wednesday CPI could send the pair UP before the BoJ meeting even convenes. The BoJ bear thesis is correct. The entry is dangerous. Classic trap: right direction, punishing sequencing. The thesis does not expire — the entry does.
items.tweetB: Oil is no longer pricing Armageddon. It is pricing blocked exit. Hormuz mine clearance, infrastructure repair, diplomatic uncertainty — none goes away because Iran announced a pause in missile attacks. Central banks need durable energy relief, not a ceasefire announcement. The risk premium compresses but does not disappear.

items.title: Leopold Aschenbrenner: $100M to $20B in 2 Years — 8 People, $4B Anthropic, Network as Alpha
items.idea: Situational Awareness Fund: $100M (July 2024) to $20B today. 1000%+ returns over 2 years, 250%+ in 2026. 8 employees (4 investment). $4B single position in Anthropic. Leopold's fiancee is chief of staff to Dario Amodei. Flatmates include Dylan Patel (SemiAnalysis) and an Anthropic researcher. Value Aligned Research: $542M to $20B in 1 year, 113% returns. Average tech portfolio +20% in April/May 2026. Tiger Cubs had pedestrian 2025 returns.
items.angle: Not hedge funds — concentrated AI long books with network-position alpha. At $20B, Leopold has the scale of Tiger Cubs he is outperforming. The Cramer praise introduces inverse signal risk but more importantly: at $4B in Anthropic alone this is essentially a private equity bet wrapped in a hedge fund structure.
items.tweetA: $100M to $20B in 24 months. 8 employees. $4B in Anthropic alone. Leopold's fiancee is Anthropic's chief of staff. His flatmate runs SemiAnalysis. The returns were not from analysis or diligence — they were from being inside the information network before information became public. Network IS the alpha.
items.tweetB: Coatue. Tiger Global. D1. Billions in hyperscaler and semiconductor stocks. Pedestrian returns. Meanwhile a 26-year-old with 8 employees grew from $100M to $20B. The old model — research, diversification, risk management — is losing to conviction plus connectivity plus concentration. For now.

items.title: Crypto Portfolio +25% vs BTC -5.2% Since February — AI and Privacy Coins Carry Everything
items.idea: Zeneca33 portfolio +25% since Feb 26 while BTC is -5.2%. Outperformers: ZEC, TAO, HYPE, VVV/DIEM. Underperformers: REKT, BANKR. General crypto bear from quantum computing fears and MicroStrategy unrealized losses. Zcash Ironwood upgrade targeting July — enforces bound on circulating ZEC supply.
items.angle: AI/privacy tokens with real fundamental catalysts are the alpha pocket in a crypto bear market. TAO (decentralized AI compute), HYPE (fee/buyback engine), ZEC (hard supply bound). These behave like stocks with moats vs story tokens.
items.tweetA: Crypto bear market. BTC down 5.2% since February. One portfolio: +25%. What carried it? ZEC, TAO, HYPE, VVV. AI coins and privacy assets with real fundamental catalysts. The alpha in crypto right now is in tokens that behave like stocks with cash flows, not currencies with vibes.
items.tweetB: Zcash Ironwood upgrade in July: enforces a hard bound on circulating ZEC supply. Privacy tech with hard supply limits, real demand, and upcoming catalyst. This is what a crypto value investment looks like: cheap, catalyst-loaded, and mostly ignored by the narrative crowd.

items.title: Dalio Moved to Dubai — Stage 5 of 6 Toward Monetary Breakdown — Fourth Turning: Who Wins
items.idea: Ray Dalio: US at stage 5 of 6 on Fourth Turning cycle, progressing toward monetary and societal breakdown. Moved to Dubai. Three options: fight for a side, keep your head down, or flee. Fourth Turning beneficiaries: builders over financiers, producers over speculators, real assets, industrial capacity, energy, food, metals, defense. Financialized elites and over-leveraged systems lose.
items.angle: Dalio's personal move is more informative than his book. The investment thesis is anti-AI hype, pro-real assets. These two worldviews cannot both be priced correctly simultaneously — the question is sequencing.
items.tweetA: Ray Dalio wrote a book saying stage 5 of 6 leads to monetary breakdown. Then he moved to Dubai. Most people read the book. Fewer noticed the move. When your macro framework for the US becomes your personal relocation decision, that is not commentary — that is conviction with skin in the game.
items.tweetB: Fourth Turning investing thesis: real assets, industrial capacity, energy, food, metals, defense win. Financialized elites and over-leveraged systems lose. This is the exact opposite of the current AI mania thesis. Both cannot be priced correctly at the same time.

items.title: No AI Jobs Crisis in the Data: NFP +172K, Openings Above 1.0 — But Glassdoor Confidence at Record Low
items.idea: Apollo/Slok: job openings per unemployed worker back above 1.0. May NFP +172K. No sign of AI replacing workers. Glassdoor Employee Confidence: 43.5% positive 6-month outlook in May — second consecutive record low. Workers feel frozen in job market.
items.angle: Macro data and micro experience in direct contradiction. NFP says fine; Glassdoor says record fear. Three explanations: irrational fear, lagging data, or the freeze effect suppressing turnover while suffering rises.
items.tweetA: Apollo: job openings per unemployed worker back above 1.0, NFP +172K. No sign of AI replacing workers. Glassdoor: employee confidence at a record low for the second straight month. Both are true simultaneously. The data says fine. The people doing the jobs say something completely different.
items.tweetB: If AI replaces white-collar work, you would expect job openings to collapse first, unemployment to rise second. Slok says we are not seeing either. But Glassdoor is at a record low. Maybe we are in the fear cycle before the displacement cycle begins. The lag between AI fear and AI impact could be measured in years.

charts.desc: Job openings per unemployed worker chart — inflection back above 1.0

items.title: After 1 Year of Tariffs: Limited Evidence of Reshoring — Supply Chains Rerouting, Not Rebuilding
items.idea: Morgan Stanley: after one full year of tariffs, supply chain reorientation (rerouting trade flows) far exceeds reshoring (building domestic capacity). Genuine reshoring requires ecosystem, labor, capital, and up-front investment. AI-led investments may provide a path but not yet visible in data.
items.angle: Tariffs create friction, not factories. Political narrative says tariffs work. Supply chain data says companies found workarounds. Inflationary effect is immediate; productive reindustrialization is a decade away.
items.tweetA: Morgan Stanley after one year of tariffs: limited evidence of reshoring. Companies rerouted supply chains to avoid tariffs — they did not build factories in America. Trade friction is a tax that compounds immediately. Domestic productive capacity is a 10-year project. One is happening. The other is not.
items.tweetB: Theory: tariffs raise import costs, US production becomes competitive, reshoring follows. Reality: companies found new trade routes, costs rose, no new domestic capacity built. Inflation without reindustrialization. Year 1 data says the theory is not working at the timeline politics assumed.

items.title: Sanders and Trump Both Want Government Equity Stakes in AI — Nationalization Back in Vogue
items.idea: Bernie Sanders (far left) and Donald Trump (populist right) both calling for US government equity stakes in AI companies. Bloomberg: Will AI become a public utility? Reuters: nationalization back in vogue. Context: OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs upcoming; anxiety about automation job displacement.
items.angle: Bipartisan agreement is rare and historically predictive of regulation. When opposite ends of politics converge on an industry, action follows within 12-18 months. OpenAI and Anthropic IPO into this environment. That risk premium is not in the multiples.
items.tweetA: Bernie Sanders and Donald Trump agree: the government should own equity stakes in AI companies. That sentence. When the exact opposite ends of American politics converge on an industry, regulation follows within 18 months. OpenAI and Anthropic are about to IPO into this environment. That risk is not priced.
items.tweetB: Nationalization back in vogue — Bloomberg, today, about AI. The only lobby against it is the companies themselves. Sanders plus Trump plus public fear of job displacement plus trillion-dollar AI valuations equals a regulatory story with no natural political opposition.

items.title: Why Renaissance Keeps Medallion at $10B: Capacity Is the Real Constraint on Alpha, Not Capital
items.idea: Medallion Fund: ~66% gross annually 1988-2018, capped at ~$10B. Returns profit annually to insiders to stay small. As size grows: market impact widens fills, crowding compresses patterns, turnover moves prices the model predicts. RIEF (client fund, $50B+): market returns — same signals, same firm, capacity not protected. Alpha doesn't scale.
items.angle: Every quant raising $50B+ claiming Medallion-like alpha is running RIEF and selling Medallion. The capacity question is the most important and least disclosed variable in fund selection.
items.tweetA: Medallion Fund: 66% gross annually for 30 years. Capped at $10B. Returns profit every year to stay within that cap. The best fund in history voluntarily limits its own size because the edge does not scale — adding capital widens market impact, compresses patterns, makes the model predict its own moves.
items.tweetB: The Medallion-RIEF gap is the most honest confession in finance. Same firm. Same signals. Medallion for insiders at $10B: 66% gross. RIEF for clients at $50B+: market returns. The only difference is size. Every quant raising $50B claiming Medallion-level alpha is running RIEF labeled as Medallion.

items.title: Iran Deal Hopes Push Oil Below $90; QQQ Still Needs 3.3% to Recover Last Thursday's Close
items.idea: Trump: Iran deal possible in 2-3 days. WTI crude back below $90. SPY +40bps, QQQ +80bps pre-market. QQQ rallied 1.5% Monday after 4%+ drop Friday — still needs 3.3% to return to last Thursday. IGV (Software ETF) surged 15% over 4 trading days end of May, then gave most back quickly.
items.angle: The 3.3% QQQ gap is the trade setup. Confirmed deal: gap fills fast with semis leading. No deal: pre-market bid reverses entirely. IGV 15% swing in 4 days then reversal in 3 signals fragile headline-driven rotation.
items.tweetA: Trump says Iran deal in 2-3 days. Oil below $90. QQQ +0.8% pre-market. But QQQ still needs 3.3% just to return to last Thursday close. Confirmed deal fills the gap fast with semis leading. No deal and the pre-market bid reverses. Wednesday CPI and Iran news in the next 72 hours decides.
items.tweetB: IGV (software ETF) +15% in 4 trading days, then gave most of it back in 3 days. That is not sector rotation — that is headline-driven volatility in both directions. When 4-day moves of 15% reverse in 3 days, the signal is the fragility of the underlying conviction, not the direction.

charts.desc: QQQ candlestick showing Friday drop and Monday recovery gap — worth opening
